en: I use these six idioms every day, and so should you.
ko: 매일 쓰는 관용구 6개, 너도 써야 해. 첫 번째, so

en: Number one, so far, so good.
This means everything is going well, up to now.
ko: far so good. 지금까지 잘 되고 있다는 뜻이야. 시험 절반을

en: I'm halfway through my exam, so far, so good.
Two, might as well, or may as well.
ko: 봤는데 so far so good. 둘, might as well 또는 may as well. 안 할 이유가 없으니

en: This means you should do something because there's no reason not to.
It's already late, we might as well stay up and watch the sunrise.
Three, to be out of sorts.
ko: 셋, to be out of sorts. 약간 몸이 안 좋거나

en: This means feeling slightly unwell or irritable.
Sorry I snapped at you earlier. I've been out of sorts all day.
ko: 짜증나는 상태야. 아까 짜증 부려서 미안. 하루 종일 컨디션이 안 좋았어. 넷,

en: Four, easier said than done.
This means something is more difficult to do than to talk about.
ko: easier said than done. 말은 쉽지만 하기는 어려운 거야.

en: Just relax and be yourself on your first date.
ko: 첫 데이트에서 긴장 풀고 자연스럽게 행동해.

en: Easier said than done, I guess. Five, at all costs.
ko: 말이야 쉽지. 다섯, at all costs. 아무리 어렵거나 비용이 들어도

en: This means you'll do something no matter how difficult or expensive it is.
We must finish this project by Friday, at all costs.
ko: 하겠다는 거야. 금요일까지 이 프로젝트를 무슨 수를 써서라도 끝내야 해.

en: And six, to lose track of something or someone.
ko: 여섯, to lose track of something or someone.

en: This means to become unable to remember or account for something or someone.
ko: 뭔가를 기억하거나 파악할 수 없게 되는 거야. 일이 너무 바빠서

en: I got so busy at work that I lost track of time.
Use your favourite in a sentence in the comments.
ko: 시간 가는 줄 몰랐어. 가장 좋아하는 걸로 댓글에 문장을 만들어봐.
